name: openPangu-2.0-Flash (Thinking)
archDisplay: MoE with MLA + DSA/SWA hybrid attention (1:2 ratio), 4-stream mHC residual, multi-token prediction heads; trained on Huawei Ascend NPUs (no NVIDIA hardware)
note: Announced at HDC 2026 (June 12, 2026); Huawei's first frontier-scale open-weight release, trained entirely on Ascend 910B NPUs with zero NVIDIA involvement. A larger 505B-total/18B-active 'Pro' sibling in the same openPangu 2.0 family was also announced but its benchmark card could not be independently confirmed via a fetchable source this pass (see uncertain). Scores here are the model's 'Thinking' inference-mode numbers; a 'Non-Thinking' mode is reported separately as a sibling entry per the effort-tier convention.

results.notes: Thinking mode only; no Non-Thinking score reported
PinchBench, Avg@3, Thinking mode column of the official benchmark table

verificationNotes:
Specs and benchmark table pulled directly from the official Hugging Face model card (openpangu/openPangu-2.0-Flash README_EN.md). Release date/venue cross-checked against independent HDC 2026 coverage.

2026-07-16: Re-checked all five flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, mmmu) for openPangu-2.0-Flash (Thinking). Re-fetched the raw official model card (huggingface.co/openpangu/openPangu-2.0-Flash/raw/main/README_EN.md) and confirmed its complete benchmark table has exactly 28 entries across General/Reasoning/Agent/Coding categories (CL-Bench, IFEval, IFBench, AgentIF, SysBench, Multichallenge, AIME 2026 [+w/Python], HMMT Feb 2025 [+w/Python], IMO-AnswerBench [+w/Python], BBEH, GPQA-Diamond, BrowseComp, SkillsBench, PinchBench, Claw-Eval, WildClawBench, MCP-Atlas, TAU2-Bench, LiveCodeBench V6, DeepCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, FeatBench) — none of MMLU, HLE, MATH-500, or HumanEval appear anywhere in it. The sibling openPangu-2.0-Flash (Non-Thinking) entry draws on the same table and has the identical gap. Checked independent leaderboards: Epoch AI's benchmarking hub has no page/entry for this model (404), and Vals.ai has no openPangu listing at all. A separate arXiv quantization study (2606.21257) does report MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval, but only for the unrelated openPangu-1B/7B dense models, not this 92B/6B-active MoE release, so it isn't a valid cross-reference. Huawei's 2.0-generation benchmark suite appears to have deliberately replaced the legacy MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval trio with newer, harder evals (GPQA-Diamond/BBEH for knowledge-reasoning, AIME/HMMT/IMO-AnswerBench for math, LiveCodeBench V6/SWE-bench Verified/DeepCodeBench/FeatBench for coding) rather than reporting saturated legacy numbers — a legitimate, expected omission for a mid-2026 frontier release, not a data gap on our end. mmlu, hle, math, and humaneval are therefore left omitted_no_evidence. mmmu is additionally structurally inapplicable: this model's modalities are text-only (capabilities.vision = false) and MMMU is an image-grounded multimodal benchmark, so no vision-capable evaluation of this model could exist to report.

2026-07-18: Fresh targeted pass specifically chasing 'hle' and 'mmmu' gaps (per round-22 task), going beyond the 2026-07-16 check.

HLE — checked 6 methods, no score found anywhere:
1. Official model card: re-confirmed the raw README_EN.md's 28-entry benchmark table (General/Reasoning/Agent/Coding categories) contains no HLE row for either Thinking or Non-Thinking mode.
2. Independent trackers: fetched BenchLM's HLE leaderboard (benchlm.ai/benchmarks/hle, 44 models — Anthropic/Meta/OpenAI/Moonshot/Z.AI/Xiaomi/InternScience/Alibaba/Google/DeepSeek/xAI/NVIDIA — no Huawei/Pangu entry), llm-stats.com's HLE leaderboard (90 models, same result — no Pangu), and Artificial Analysis's HLE evaluations page (no Pangu entry). Epoch AI's benchmarking hub was previously confirmed 404 for this model (2026-07-16 note) and general search of epoch.ai/benchmarks turns up no openPangu HLE coverage either. Scale SEAL/Seal-0 leaderboard (July 2026, 6 models, led by Kimi K2.5) also has no Pangu entry.
3. Academic sources: checked arXiv 2512.23367 (\"Post-Training Quantization of OpenPangu Models for Efficient Deployment on Atlas A2\") — this evaluates openPangu-Embedded 1B/7B on HumanEval/MBPP only, not HLE, and not this 92B/6B-active Flash release. The previously-identified 2606.21257 quantization paper likewise only covers the unrelated dense 1B/7B models on legacy benchmarks.
4. HF discussions page for the model: only contains \"add evaluation results\", \"GGUF version\", \"Nvidia support\" threads — no community-posted HLE numbers.
5. Sibling-model comparison tables: checked comparison articles/tables for GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.6, Qwen 3.6/3.7 Max (contemporaneous releases that do report their own HLE scores, e.g. GLM 5.2 = 54.7, Kimi K2.6 = 54) — none of them include openPangu-2.0-Flash as a cited comparison baseline for HLE.
6. GitHub eval-harness search for openpangu + HLE run logs returned nothing relevant.
Conclusion: HLE remains genuinely unpublished/unreported for this model as of 2026-07-18, not merely hard to find — left omitted_no_evidence.

MMMU — reconfirmed architecturally inapplicable: corpus file's modalities = [\"text\"] and capabilities.vision = false; openPangu-2.0-Flash is a text-only MoE LLM with no vision encoder or image-input path (confirmed via HF model card architecture description). MMMU is an image-grounded multimodal benchmark and cannot be administered to a text-only model, so no real score could exist to find. Left omitted_no_evidence with reason \"architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)\".

No corpus edits made this pass — both gaps confirmed as legitimate non-findings rather than added to results[].
